req.title: Open checking account (negative variant)
req.statement: A verified customer shall be able to open a checking account with an initial deposit between $25 and $50,000; deposits outside this range shall be rejected. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines a deposit range of $25–$50,000 with three distinct error pathways. Test design covers: (1) EP – a valid mid-range deposit by a verified customer (positive); (2) BVA – exact lower boundary $25 and upper boundary $50,000 accepted, and the first invalid values $24 and $50,001 rejected; (3) NEG – deposit below minimum, deposit above maximum, and unverified customer attempt. A DT case captures the combination of unverified customer state to exercise the 403 pathway. State Transition Coverage is applied to confirm the account moves from 'pending' to 'active' on success and remains absent on rejection. Each test uses isolated fixtures and unique idempotency keys to remain deterministic and repeatable.

tests.name: test_open_checking_valid_deposit_mid_range
actions: POST /accounts/checking with verified auth token and deposit=1000
trace: R-FIN-051: verified customer; deposit in valid range; account created; notification dispatched

tests.name: test_open_checking_deposit_below_minimum_rejected
actions: POST /accounts/checking with verified auth token and deposit=10
expected: response body contains error code indicating deposit below minimum
trace: R-FIN-051: deposit < 25 must be rejected with 422

tests.name: test_open_checking_deposit_above_maximum_rejected
actions: POST /accounts/checking with verified auth token and deposit=75000
expected: response body contains error indicating exceeded deposit limit
trace: R-FIN-051: deposit > 50000 must be rejected with 422

tests.name: test_open_checking_unverified_customer_forbidden
preconditions: deposit amount $500 is within valid range
actions: POST /accounts/checking with unverified auth token and deposit=500
trace: R-FIN-051: unverified customer must receive 403 regardless of deposit amount

tests.name: test_open_checking_deposit_at_lower_boundary_accepted
preconditions: deposit amount $25 is exactly the lower boundary
actions: POST /accounts/checking with verified auth token and deposit=25
trace: R-FIN-051: deposit == 25 (lower boundary) must be accepted

tests.name: test_open_checking_deposit_one_below_lower_boundary_rejected
preconditions: deposit amount $24 is one unit below lower boundary $25
actions: POST /accounts/checking with verified auth token and deposit=24
expected: response body contains deposit validation error
trace: R-FIN-051: deposit == 24 (lower boundary - 1) must be rejected with 422

tests.name: test_open_checking_deposit_at_upper_boundary_accepted
preconditions: deposit amount $50000 is exactly the upper boundary
actions: POST /accounts/checking with verified auth token and deposit=50000
trace: R-FIN-051: deposit == 50000 (upper boundary) must be accepted

tests.name: test_open_checking_deposit_one_above_upper_boundary_rejected
preconditions: deposit amount $50001 is one unit above upper boundary $50000
actions: POST /accounts/checking with verified auth token and deposit=50001
expected: response body contains deposit limit exceeded error
trace: R-FIN-051: deposit == 50001 (upper boundary + 1) must be rejected with 422

tests.name: test_open_checking_account_state_transitions_to_active
preconditions: deposit amount $500 is within valid range
no prior checking account exists for this customer session
actions: POST /accounts/checking with verified auth token and deposit=500
expected: POST returns HTTP 201 with status='pending' or status='active'
account was not in 'active' state before the POST
trace: R-FIN-051: account state must transition from non-existent to active after successful account opening
